channel.title: AISight — AI Capital Expenditure Tracker
channel.description: Latest news and data on AI infrastructure spending across 38 companies. Updated 3x daily from SEC filings and earnings calls.

item.title: Tech giants ramp up AI spending, but returns remain uncertain: Jefferies
item.description: The report “The Mother of All Capex Cycles” notes that AI-related capital expenditure by major US tech companies could reach $700 billion this year and $800 billion next year, highlighting a significant investment surge

item.title: Zuckerberg tells Meta employees the layoffs are about capex, not AI productivity
item.description: At a Thursday town hall, the Meta CEO made the explicit trade-off he had previously left implicit: ‘We basically have two major cost centres in the company: compute infrastructure and people-oriented things.’ The chief people officer also declined to rule out… [Meta]

item.title: Apple posted its best quarter ever by not building an AI model
item.description: Apple reported its best March quarter in history on Thursday, posting revenue of $111.2 billion, up 17 per cent year over year, with net profit of $29.6 billion. iPhone revenue reached a March quarter record of $58 billion, up 22 per cent, driven by what Tim … [Apple]

item.title: AI lifts clouds even higher, AWS moves up the stack, and Elon and Sam battle in court
item.description: If anyone thought artificial intelligence was running into a slow patch, let alone running into any fundamental technical or financial hurdles, this week quieted the doubters — at least for now. In a blockbuster week of Big Tech earnings, Alphabet, Amazon, Mi… [Amazon, Alphabet, Intel, ServiceNow]

item.title: Reddit Q1 revenue jumps 69% to $663m, and shares rally 9%
item.description: The social platform beat Wall Street estimates across every line, raised Q2 guidance above consensus, and reported $1m in capital expenditure, a striking contrast to the hyperscaler capex arms race. Reddit reported first-quarter 2026 revenue of $663 million, …

item.title: Top CEO pay increased 20 times faster than workers’ pay in 2025
item.description:
• Global real worker pay fell 12 percent while real CEO pay surged 54 percent between 2019 and 2025.
  • At least four CEOs of major corporations each pocketed over $100 million in pay and bonuses last year. Broadcom CEO Hock Tan led the pack at ove… [Broadcom]

item.title: The US government and the AI industry are both betting on debt. They are drawing from the same pool.
item.description: The United States government now owes more than the country produces. Data released by the Bureau of Economic Analysis on 30 April showed that debt held by the public stood at $31.27 trillion at the end of March, while nominal GDP over the preceding 12 months… [ServiceNow]

item.title: Big Tech Q1 2026 earnings: Key takeaways on AI spending, cloud growth, and more
item.description: From accelerated cloud revenue growth to rising AI-related capex, layoffs, and more, here’s a look at the key takeaways from Big Tech’s latest quarterly earnings results.

item.title: Atlassian stock soars 28% after earnings show strong cloud, data center growth
item.description: Atlassian's stock has been hit hard in the "SaaS-pocalypse" sweeping software names as AI threatens to disrupt their business models.

item.title: ChatGPT OpenAI Elon Musk trial: From calling Artificial Intelligence firm charity to existential crisis, key takeaways from Tesla boss' testimony
